question_title: Will WEC's market close price on 2026-03-12 be higher than its market close price on 2026-03-03?
summary: WEC Energy Group is in a clear uptrend, rising from ~$103 to ~$117 over the past 60 days. Monte Carlo simulations using empirical volatility (12.6% annualized) give P(up over 7 trading days) ranging from 50% (zero drift) to 70% (recent drift), with a blended estimate of 63%. The stock is only 1.3% below its 52-week high with RSI at 69 (not yet overbought), suggesting momentum can persist. However, the recent drift rate of 0.16%/day (39% annualized) is unsustainably high, and the broader S&P 500 has been flat/slightly weak. Settling at 55% as the final estimate anchors on the blended Monte Carlo result with a modest adjustment for momentum persistence factors.

factors.description: Unconditional base rate for stock being up over 7 trading days is ~52%
WEC in strong uptrend: 20-day return +6.3%, 60-day trend from $106 to $117. Monte Carlo with recent drift gives P(up)=70%, blended gives 63%
Stock near 52-week high (1.3% below), RSI ~69 - not overbought, momentum persistence signal in low-vol utility
Historical 7-day forward return win rate of 81% over 53 overlapping windows (reduced effective sample, but directionally informative)
Broader market (S&P 500) flat/slightly weak recently, could limit upside
Mean reversion risk: 60-day drift of 0.16%/day (39% annualized) is unsustainably high

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.65 (65%)**

WEC Energy Group has strong momentum — up 6.3% over 20 days, near its 52-week high, with RSI at 69 (not yet overbought). The Monte Carlo simulation using blended drift (half recent momentum, half long-run equity return) gives 63% probability of being higher after 7 trading days. The technical setup supports trend continuation in this low-volatility utility stock. However, the recent drift rate (39% annualized) is unsustainably high and the broader market is flat, warranting some caution. The 65% estimate properly weights the quantitative evidence while acknowledging mean reversion risk.
